content: import { ApiTable } from "@/components/api-table.tsx"; import { Example } from "@/components/example.tsx"; import { Figure } from "@/components/figure.tsx"; export const title = "pointer-events"; export const description = "Utilities for controlling whether an element responds to pointer events."; ## Examples ### Ignoring pointer events Use the `pointer-events-none` utility to make an element ignore pointer events, like `:hover` and `click` events:

assName="mb-3 font-mono text-xs font-medium text-gray-500 dark:text-gray-400">pointer-events-none

} ```html
``` The pointer events will still trigger on child elements and pass-through to elements that are "beneath" the target. ### Restoring pointer events Use the `pointer-events-auto` utility to revert to the default browser behavior for pointer events: ```html
